title: Shri Banke Bihari Temple, Vrindavan
intro: The Shri Banke Bihari Temple is one of the holiest and most visited temples in Vrindavan, dedicated to Lord Krishna. The idol here was discovered by Swami Haridas, a legendary devotee and musician.
description:
Shri Banke Bihari Temple – Vrindavan, Uttar Pradesh
Introduction
The Shri Banke Bihari Temple is one of the holiest and most visited temples in Vrindavan, dedicated to Lord Krishna. The idol here was discovered by Swami Haridas, a legendary devotee and musician. The temple is unique because it follows a very intimate form of worship; there are no bells or conch shells used, as the deity is treated like a beloved child who should not be disturbed by loud noises.
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Shri Banke Bihari Temple
 Location: Vrindavan, Mathura District, Uttar Pradesh 281121
 Presiding Deity: Lord Krishna (Banke Bihari form)
 Significance: Famous for the unique "curtain" system (Jhanki) where the deity is not shown continuously.
History and Significance
The temple was established based on the discovery of the idol by Swami Haridas in Nidhivan. The idol is said to have appeared before him upon his prayer. The temple remains a focal point for devotees of Krishna, and the spiritual energy here is considered very intense, especially during the festive season of Holi and Janmashtami.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air:
 Agra Airport (Kheria): Approx. 70 km away.
 Delhi (IGI Airport): Approx. 150 km away (well-connected by road/rail).
🚆 By Train:
 Mathura Junction: The nearest major railway station, well-connected to all major Indian cities. Taxis and autos are available from here to Vrindavan (approx. 12 km).
🛣️ By Road:
 Vrindavan is easily accessible via the Yamuna Expressway from Delhi and Agra.
Best Time to Visit
 October to March: The weather is cool and comfortable.
 Festivals: Janmashtami (birth of Krishna) and Holi (Lathmar Holi) are the best times to experience the vibrant culture of Vrindavan.
Temple Timings
 Summer: Morning (7:45 AM – 1:00 PM) | Evening (5:30 PM – 9:30 PM)
 Winter: Morning (8:45 AM – 1:00 PM) | Evening (4:30 PM – 8:30 PM)
(Note: Timings are subject to change based on seasons and specific ritual requirements.)
Nearby Attractions
 Prem Mandir: A stunning white marble temple known for its evening light shows.
 ISKCON Temple (Sri Krishna-Balaram Mandir): Famous for its peaceful atmosphere and kirtans.
 Nidhivan: A mysterious forest said to be the site of Lord Krishna's Raas Leela.
 Mathura Janmabhoomi: The birthplace of Lord Krishna.
Dress Code
 As it is a sacred religious site, wearing modest and traditional attire is recommended.
Facilities for Devotees
 Queue management system.
 Prasad counters.
 Proximity to various dharamshalas and hotels.
Conclusion
A visit to the Banke Bihari Temple is a transformative experience for any devotee. The unique atmosphere, the singing of bhajans, and the divine presence of Banke Bihari make it an essential destination for anyone traveling through the Braj region.

faq.q1: To which deity is the Banke Bihari Temple dedicated?
faq.a1: This temple is dedicated to the Banke Bihari form of Lord Shri Krishna.
faq.q2: Where is the Banke Bihari Temple located?
faq.a2: It is located in Vrindavan, Mathura district, Uttar Pradesh.
faq.q3: What is the Banke Bihari Temple famous for?
faq.a3: It is famous for the profound devotion to Lord Krishna, its unique worship traditions (such as the absence of temple bells), and the unwavering faith of devotees.
faq.q4: What is the best time to visit the Banke Bihari Temple?
faq.a4: The period from October to March is considered ideal for travel due to the pleasant weather.
faq.q5: What are the places to see in Vrindavan?
faq.a5: Major places to visit include Prem Mandir, ISKCON Temple, Nidhivan, and the Shri Krishna Janmabhoomi (in Mathura).
